evals.prompt: Research this URL and create a reference document for me: https://core.telegram.org/bots/api#available-methods — I need a clean reference of the Telegram Bot API available methods that I can use as context when building my bot.
evals.expected_output: A well-structured markdown file covering Telegram Bot API methods with accurate parameter tables, code examples, and return types
expectations: Output is one or more .md files
Contains a metadata header with source URL and fetch date
Preserves API method signatures and parameter details accurately
Includes parameter tables with name, type, required/optional, and description
Does not contain hallucinated methods or parameters not in the source
Strips navigation/non-content elements from the source page

evals.prompt: I need to understand Go's context package for my bot project. Research https://pkg.go.dev/context and create a reference doc I can use.
evals.expected_output: A single markdown file covering Go context package: overview, core types, functions, usage patterns, and examples
expectations: Output is a single .md file (focused topic)
Contains a metadata header with source URL and fetch date
Accurately describes Context interface and its methods
Includes WithCancel, WithDeadline, WithTimeout, WithValue functions
Preserves code examples from the source
Notes about context cancellation propagation are accurate

evals.prompt: Research https://go.dev/doc/effective_go and save it as reference docs. This is a big document so split it into multiple files if that makes sense.
evals.expected_output: Multiple markdown files organized by section (formatting, control structures, functions, data, concurrency, etc.) with an index file linking them
expectations: Output is split into multiple .md files (document is large enough to warrant splitting)
An index/README file exists that links to all sub-files
Each sub-file has a metadata header with source URL
Code examples are preserved exactly as in the source
Covers major sections: formatting, commentary, names, control structures, functions, data, concurrency, errors
No content is hallucinated — all text traces to the source document
